title: 3D completion and overlay parity
description: Bring the required 3D capability to v0.1 acceptance: base view availability, lazy bundle/fallback behavior, drag rotate, pan, wheel zoom, reset, fit, visible gizmo, zone/layer/kind placement, and auth/dataflow/boundary/permission/validation overlay parity where practical.
triage_notes: Cut from product principles §6, 03 3D view, and 04 engine acceptance. Completion: core fallback remains, real 3D bundle passes interaction/framing tests, and overlay-relevant model elements are visible or gracefully degraded in 3D. Use test/fixtures/pattern-samples as acceptance input for 3D/isometric framing and overlay parity because the set covers small, medium, large hybrid, and Android architectures. 2026-06-27 Codex update: playground/viewer controls now expose overview/layer and 2d/3d only; the playground template includes auth/dataflow/boundary/permission/validation metadata; 3D consumes overlay projection for emphasized edges, permission overlay edges, node badges, boundary volumes, icons, and corner-offset zone labels. 2026-06-27 Codex update: 2D routing now treats component-border coincidence as a hard route cost and regression; ports are redistributed across opposite faces, endpoint lanes are deconflicted, fallback safety routes prefer distributed ports, and final endpoint/bend cleanup preserves no-node-hit/no-border-hit invariants. Verified on comprehensive + pattern samples: borderHits=0 across all checked fixtures. 2026-06-27 Codex update: added rendered SVG port validation plumbing (src/render-validation.ts) and wired it into pattern CLI/browser verifiers. Validation checks exact endpoint overlap per node, same-side minimum port gap, missing rendered node metadata, long same-line segment overlap, and component-region intersections per rendered base view + overlay combination; path segment parsing respects SVG M breaks. Routing now repairs component-intersecting edges with shortest valid orthogonal routes across candidate source/target ports, then decongests final endpoints. Permission overlay lines are summarized into target-node badges instead of adding dense synthetic routes. Verification: npm test (141 passing), npm run build, npm run verify:pattern-samples (6 samples x 3 base views x 32 overlay sets = 576 2D renders, failures=[]). 2026-06-27 Codex update: added endpoint-incidence validation requiring rendered source/target segments to meet the touched component side perpendicularly. Layout now enforces final normal stubs at both source and target, with orthogonal connector points so SVG path construction preserves the perpendicular incidence. Verification repeated: npm run build, npm run verify:pattern-samples (576 renders, failures=[]), npm test (141 passing). 2026-06-27 Codex update: layer view now uses fixed horizontal layer bands instead of zone boxes. Android extension metadata (`androidComponent`, `androidLayer`) is parsed and mapped to platform stack bands: Applications, Application Framework, Libraries (user space), Linux Kernel, and Baseband; non-Android diagrams still use standard ArchMap layers. Verification: npm run typecheck, npm test (142 passing), npm run build, npm run verify:pattern-samples (576 renders, failures=[]). 2026-06-28 Codex update: zone is now treated as Add info rather than a primary viewer base view; overview is structural by default and zone boxes render via the `zone` overlay. Layer view now renders graph subgraphs as grouping boxes over fixed layer bands so Mermaid-style subgraph intent remains visible in layer diagrams. Pattern verifiers now cover overview/layer x 64 overlay sets, including `zone`, for 768 2D renders. Final route repair now re-runs component-safe routing with endpoint-use penalties after perpendicular stubs so layer diagrams keep no-overlap/no-node-hit guarantees. Verification: npm test (142 passing), npm run build, npm run verify:pattern-samples (768 renders, failures=[]). 2026-06-28 Codex update: subgraph is now an Add info overlay alongside zone and boundary, so overview and layer base views no longer force subgraph/zone/boundary area boxes by default. Layer remains fixed layer bands; subgraph, zone, and boundary can be toggled independently on any 2D base view. Playground/viewer and pattern verification overlay lists include `subgraph`. Verification for this turn was scoped to the affected surface after user guidance to avoid always running the full render matrix: npm run typecheck, targeted render/pattern tests, npm test (143 passing), npm run build. The full pattern verifier was expanded to cover 7 overlays but intentionally stopped before completion this turn because it is too heavy for routine changes; reserve it for routing/overlay/verifier changes or periodic QIF checks. 2026-06-28 Codex update: clarified the visual taxonomy after human review. `subgraph` is now authoring-only hierarchy preserved in the model and no longer an Add info overlay or implicit zone source. Add info remains fixed renderer categories: zone, boundary, auth, dataflow, permission, validation. Zones represent physical component areas and can nest via `parent`/zone contains; boundaries represent logical component areas and now render nested boundary boxes with depth. The layer base view is presented as Stack in UI while retaining `baseView: "layer"` compatibility; stack partitioning comes from `nodes.*.layer` and is not affected by zone/boundary overlays. Verification: npm run typecheck, targeted parser/layout/render/pattern tests, npm run build. 2026-06-28 Codex update: controls are now actual HTML tag controls in playground/static demo and `<archmap-viewer controls>`. Views and render modes use radio inputs, Add info remains additive checkbox tags, and tag panels support minimize/expand actions. 3D now also exposes HTML Top/Front/Side gizmo buttons in addition to the existing axis helper click behavior. Verification: npm run typecheck, npm run build, npm test -- --run test/render.test.ts test/scene3d.test.ts. 2026-06-28 Codex update: Add info toggles no longer change the current 2D zoom/pan. RenderResult overlay updates preserve the existing panZoom transform, and the playground/static demo now call addOverlay/removeOverlay instead of reparsing and recreating the render result on every Add info click. Verification: npm run typecheck, npm test -- --run test/interaction.test.ts test/render.test.ts, npm run build. 2026-06-28 Codex update: removed the extra HTML Top/Front/Side 3D buttons so camera snapping remains on the lower-right ViewHelper cube. The playground/static demo canvas area is taller for 3D, and both 2D/3D now expose a Full/Exit tag action; <archmap-viewer controls> also includes a Full action using the browser Fullscreen API. Verification: npm run typecheck, npm test -- --run test/render.test.ts test/scene3d.test.ts, npm run build. 2026-06-28 Codex update: replaced the 3D corner axis helper with a labeled Fusion-style ViewCube in the lower-right corner; clicking faces labeled 正面/上面/右側 snaps the camera. Replaced confusing +/- tag panel controls with explicit SVG panel expand/minimize icons in playground/static demo and <archmap-viewer controls>. Browser check on 127.0.0.1:4174 confirmed one canvas, one ViewCube, labels present, icon buttons have no +/- text, and 正面 click emits no console errors. Verification: npm run typecheck, npm test -- --run test/render.test.ts test/scene3d.test.ts, npm run build. 2026-06-28 Codex update: kept the labeled ViewCube visual but restored the camera snap feel by animating face-click transitions instead of jumping the camera immediately. OrbitControls drag/pan/zoom settings remain unchanged. Browser check on 127.0.0.1:4174 confirmed one 3D canvas, one ViewCube, 上面 click, and no console errors. Verification: npm run typecheck, npm test -- --run test/scene3d.test.ts, npm run build. 2026-06-28 Codex update: removed the 3D ground plane and grid so the 3D view is a clean spatial architecture map. Added docs/aof/OVERLAY-SEMANTICS-2026-06-28.md to pin the intended responsibilities for auth/dataflow/boundary/permission/validation before continuing overlay cleanup: auth = token lifecycle, dataflow = data objects/classification/storage, boundary = crossings/declared boundaries, permission = principal-resource access, validation = diagnostics. Browser check on 127.0.0.1:4174 confirmed one 3D canvas, one ViewCube, and no console errors. Verification: npm run typecheck, npm test -- --run test/scene3d.test.ts, npm run build.
